summary: Backend-only regression and observability validation completed for startup/execution/recovery/deploy-readiness scope. Official probe suite passed, targeted probes passed, and added BUY entry-price guard probe passed.
action_items: Optional hardening: include /app/tests/buy_entry_price_guard_probe.ts into scripts/run-probes.mjs so BUY entry-price guard becomes an official regression gate.
Keep startup phase logs and worker timeout recovery probes as mandatory CI checks.
Preserve env contract parity between src/config/env.ts and .env.example on every env-related change.
critical_code_review_comments: ExecutionEngine remains very large (>1700 LOC), increasing regression risk for trading/recovery logic despite current passing behavior.
Standalone probe execution requires explicit runtime envs (expected by current contract); using scripts/run-probes.mjs is the reliable path.
Probe integrations for exchange/telegram are partly MOCKED by design; this is appropriate for safe regression but not proof of real live-exchange behavior.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data. Probes used isolated temporary DATA_DIR.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Executed successfully: yarn typecheck:probes, yarn build, node scripts/run-probes.mjs, yarn test:probes, and targeted buy_entry_price_guard_probe. Requested scope validated: bootstrap error clarity on bind failure, startup phase logging, worker timeout recovery without false shutdown error noise, Indodax recovery probes, official probe runner coverage for bootstrap_observability_probe + worker_timeout_probe, and env example/runtime contract sync. Note: exchange/telegram paths in probes include MOCKED components by design.
rca of the issue: No defect reproduced in requested scope after verification. Additional RCA test added to explicitly prove BUY entry invalid path is blocked before order creation.
